justification: As the operator, A is materially more useful. A separates 'ghost listings' (Cyk Pyk last review Oct 2020, Socialife Dec 2021) from genuine 2025/2026 rapid failures (Beggin registered March 4 2026, Brassica two-month exit, Cannolo&more Dec 2025) — a distinction B blurs by lumping pandemic-era closures into the current contraction. A also surfaces the structural drivers I can actually act on: the rowerowy rebus pedestrian friction, 200,000 PLN key money benchmarks, the Flow/Plastry review-sentiment warning that locals are price-sensitive, and the Plastry Wolt-vs-UberEats 38/40 PLN cross-platform tactic. B's strongest contribution is the NEON Starowiejska direct-threat framing and the 12% delivery markup math, but it dispatched only three specialists (skipping review_analyst) and drew on roughly half the domains. B reads like a competitor brief; A reads like an operating environment scan.
spot_check_a: Beggin registered 2026-03-04 with zero Google reviews then closed — consistent with 'rapid failure' narrative; Plastry 38 PLN UberEats vs 40 PLN Wolt = ~5% delta, matches the stated cross-platform commission offset.
spot_check_b: NEON Pad Thai 51 PLN dine-in vs Monsun Dan Dan Beef 50 PLN dine-in / 56 PLN Wolt = 12% markup — math is internally consistent (56/50 = 1.12).
